All images, locations, names, and descriptions MUST be REAL, VERIFIED, and TRUSTED.

ABSOLUTELY FORBIDDEN:
- AI-generated images
- Stock photos not related to the real location
- Random Google images without verification
- Guessing descriptions or landmarks
- Fabricated ratings or places

Images MUST be sourced ONLY from:

1. Official Haramain platforms:

   - https://alharamain.gov.sa/public
   - https://alharamain.gov.sa/public/?page=nusuk-ar

2. Google Maps / Google Places:
   - Verified place photos
   - Only for locations inside Makkah & Madinah Haram context

3. Booking.com:
   - Hotels images ONLY from official hotel listings
   - No user-generated or scraped content

4. Official tourism & government platforms (Saudi):
   - No blogs
   - No forums
   - No Wikipedia images

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

For EVERY image used in the app:
- It must match the exact location shown
- It must reflect the real environment
- It must not mislead pilgrims
- Haram images must respect sanctity (no inappropriate angles)

Each image card must include (internally, not visible to user):
- Source URL
- Source type (Official / Google Maps / Booking)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

- Must exist physically
- Must be inside Haram boundaries
- Must be validated using:
  • Official Haramain maps
  • Google Maps coordinates
- No approximation
- No “nearby” guessing

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

All descriptions must be:
- Neutral
- Informational
- Respectful
- Based on official wording

DO NOT:
- Rewrite religious rulings in casual language
- Add opinions
- Add interpretations

If data is unavailable:
- Clearly mark as “Information not available from official sources”

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

If any image, location, or information cannot be verified
→ DO NOT include it in the app.

1️⃣5️⃣ ADVANCED 3D UI / UX DESIGN (CRITICAL)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• Zero clutter
• Every screen must feel intentional

Color system:
• White base
• Gold accent (#D4AF37)
• Black typography
• Soft gray separators

3D VISUAL STYLE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Implement a **pseudo-3D style** using:

• Image depth blur
• Subtle gradients
• Soft shadows
• Motion on interaction

NO cartoon style.
NO fake 3D models.
Only REAL images + depth illusion.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• Welcome message (localized)
• Smart shortcuts (cards with images):

  - Sa’i Counter
  - Nearest Services
  - AI Assistant
  - Tourist Guide

• Subtle shadow

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Rename section to:
🟡 “Tawaf Counter”

Layout:
• Large circular counter (center)
• Gold ring animation
• Each lap increments visually
• Haptic feedback (if available)

Controls:
• Start
• Pause
• Reset

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• A collapsible arrow (⬇️)

When expanded:
• Smooth slide-down animation
• Clear sections:
  - Where Tawaf starts
  - Where it ends
  - Number of laps
  - Conditions
• Button:
  “Official Guide – Source”
  (opens WebView)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SA’I COUNTER – DUAL DIRECTION LOGIC
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Rename correctly:
🟡 “Sa’i Counter”

Design:
• Two large arrow buttons:
  → Safa → Marwah
  ← Marwah → Safa

Each arrow press:
• Counts as 1 lap
• Visually updates progress
• Direction-aware

Progress display:
• Step cards with numbers
• Visual indicator of current position
• Gold highlight for active lap

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Below the counter:
• Arrow to expand

Expanded content:
• Explanation text:
  - Safa → Marwah = 1
  - Marwah → Safa = 1
• Visual step cards
• Button:
  “Explain Sa’i (Official Source)”

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The user should NEVER feel lost.

Implement:

• Back button always visible
• No locked scrolling
• No dead ends

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MICRO-INTERACTIONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• Card hover/tap response
• Smooth fade between screens
• Gentle motion on arrows

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ACCESSIBILITY VISUAL ADAPTATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• Visually impaired:
  - Larger buttons
  - High contrast
  - Voice cues
• Wheelchair:
  - Ground-floor emphasis
  - Flat routes highlighted
• Hearing impaired:
  - Clear text alerts
  - No sound dependency

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FINAL UX QUALITY BAR
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This app must feel:
• Calm
• Trustworthy
• Premium
• Spiritual
• Modern

If any screen feels empty, flat, or basic:
→ Redesign it.

No shortcuts.
No placeholders.
No unfinished UI.

Deliver a **flagship-level experience**.

**Defining App Requirements**

I'm currently focused on defining the core requirements for "Rafeeq Al-Haramain". The application will be a managed Expo project using React Native and TypeScript, with Expo Router for navigation and AsyncStorage for data persistence. Multi-language support (i18next) is crucial, alongside RTL/LTR handling.


**Visualizing Core Aesthetics**

I've been envisioning the premium UI, centering on a white base with gold accents and black typography. I'm aiming for 3D-style layered cards with depth, shadows, and motion to create a visually rich experience. I'm focusing on key features like a circular Tawaf counter and dual-direction Sa'i counter. For the AI component, I am planning to employ `gemini-3-pro-preview` for sophisticated tasks, `gemini-3-flash-preview` for search-grounded responses, and `gemini-2.5-flash` for the mapping functionality.

I'm starting with a `metadata.json` file for the app's basic information and permissions. I am implementing boilerplate React web environment code as per the instructions, with `index.html`, `index.tsx`, and `App.tsx` files.


**Resolving Tech Stack Conflict**

I've hit a snag, realizing a conflict between React Native (requested by the user) and React web with Tailwind (demanded by instructions). My focus now is on resolving this dissonance. I'm choosing to follow the system instructions. I plan to use React web and Tailwind, but simulate a mobile-like experience through responsive design and UI choices to fulfill the user's needs. I'm focusing on ensuring functional requirements are met within this web framework.

Rafeeq Al-Haramain
(رفيق الحرمين)

You are a senior mobile architect, senior React Native engineer, UX/UI designer, accessibility expert, and AI systems engineer.

Your task is to BUILD FROM SCRATCH a complete, production-ready, professional Expo React Native (TypeScript) mobile application.

This application is a full Hajj & Umrah companion app designed for pilgrims in Makkah and Madinah.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ABSOLUTE RULES (VERY IMPORTANT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

• I cannot upload any files
• You must rely 100% on this description
• You must generate the FULL project
• You must generate ALL files with FULL code
• You must NOT ask any follow-up questions
• You must NOT skip any feature
• You must NOT simplify logic
• You must NOT invent data
• You must NOT generate fake images
• NO comments inside code
• One single full execution only

If any information is missing → do NOT guess → exclude it safely.

1. CORE PURPOSE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The app is a trusted, calm, professional companion for:
• Hajj performers
• Umrah performers
• Visitors of Makkah
• Visitors of Madinah

The app must feel:
• Official
• Trustworthy
• Spiritual
• Modern
• Premium (similar or better than Nusuk)

• Expo (Managed Workflow)
• React Native
• TypeScript
• Expo Router
• AsyncStorage
• react-i18next
• In-app map rendering (no forced external navigation)
• WebView for official sources
• Clean scalable architecture

The app must run using:
npm install
npx expo start

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
3. LANGUAGES (FULL – NOT TWO)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Language selector must be:
• ONE dropdown
• Searchable
• No multiple checkboxes
• Proper RTL / LTR handling

Supported languages:
• Arabic (RTL)
• English
• French
• Urdu
• Indonesian
• Turkish
• Malay
• Bengali
• Hausa

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
4. ONBOARDING FLOW (VERY IMPORTANT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 1 – Language selection
• Single searchable dropdown
• Clean layout

STEP 2 – User Category (EXACT OPTIONS ONLY)
• Umrah Performer – Makkah
• Hajj Performer – Makkah
• Visitor – Makkah
• Visitor – Madinah

STEP 3 – Name
Ask politely:
“What would you like us to call you?”
Optional.

STEP 4 – Accessibility (REAL IMPLEMENTATION)
User selects ONE option:

• Visually impaired:
  - Voice guidance
  - Audio buttons
  - Screen reader friendly
  - Larger UI elements

• Wheelchair user:
  - Flat routes highlighted
  - Ground floors prioritized
  - Elevators marked
  - No stairs suggested

• Hearing impaired:
  - Text-only alerts
  - No audio dependency

• No disability:

These choices MUST affect real behavior and UI.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Design style:
• White background
• Gold accent (#D4AF37)
• Black typography
• Rounded cards
• Icons + REAL images everywhere
• Subtle 3D depth (layers, shadows, motion)
• Smooth animations

Bottom navigation:
• Home
• Map
• Assistant
• Rituals
• Profile

Assistant appears:
• In bottom navigation
• On Home screen
(No duplication elsewhere)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Maps MUST work INSIDE the app (Uber-like behavior):

• Live user location
• Direction lines
• Distance calculation
• Navigation inside app
• Optional button: “Open in Google Maps”

NO forced redirection.

Locations must be REAL and VERIFIED.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
7. OFFICIAL LOCATION SOURCES (MANDATORY)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

You MUST use ONLY these sources for locations and services:

• https://alharamain.gov.sa
• https://alharamain.gov.sa/public/?page=home_ar
• https://alharamain.gov.sa/public/?page=nusuk-ar
• https://alharamain.gov.sa/tawaf/
• https://carts.alharamain.gov.sa/

DO NOT scrape.
DO NOT store content.
Use WebView or verified coordinates only.

**Investigating Missing Exports**

I'm currently focused on resolving the "no exported member" error in `TawafCounter.tsx`. I've identified the issue stems from an attempt to import `TAWAF_DUAS` from `constants.ts`, a file which is either missing this constant, or has not correctly exported it. My next steps involve reviewing the code in both files for correctness.


**Pinpointing Import Errors**

I've confirmed `TAWAF_DUAS` is absent in `constants.ts`. This explains the import error in `TawafCounter.tsx`. I'll explore adding `TAWAF_DUAS` to `constants.ts` and exporting it. I am not seeing other issues in `TawafCounter.tsx`, beyond this unhandled import.


**Adding Missing Constant**

I've added the `TAWAF_DUAS` array to `constants.ts`, resolving the import error. Now, moving on, I see the Gemini service is using the correct model and budget. I also see that the API key setup in `services/geminiService.ts` is not quite right. I will directly use the API Key by updating the Gemini Service, per the instructions.


**Reviewing Code for Errors**

I've been going through `geminiService.ts` again, and while reviewing the code, I realized there's a minor issue with the API key setup. It should just use `process.env.API_KEY`, not `process.env.API_KEY || ""`. However, the rest of the Gemini service looks solid, using the correct model (`gemini-3-pro-preview`) and the budget configurations. I haven't seen any other immediate errors.


**Refining API Interaction**

## 🎯 المطلوب بالضبط:
لكل مكان في القائمة، استخرج أو أنشئ البيانات التالية:

1. **الاسم العربي**  
2. **الاسم بالإنجليزية**  
3. **نوع المكان** (Hotel / Restaurant / Cafe / Mall)  
4. **المدينة** (Makkah أو Madinah)  
5. **العنوان الكامل** (استنتجه من الخريطة أو الاسم)  
6. **خط العرض (latitude)** و **خط الطول (longitude)**  
   - إذا كان الرابط لا يعمل، استنتج الإحداثيات من اسم المكان باستخدام معرفتك الجغرافية.  
7. **رقم الهاتف** (إذا لم تجده، اتركه فارغًا)  
8. **الموقع الإلكتروني** (إذا لم تجده، اتركه فارغًا)  
9. **وصف قصير** (جملة واحدة بالعربية)  
10. **التقييم** (من 1.0 إلى 5.0) - قدره بناءً على شهرة المكان  
11. **نطاق الأسعار** (مثلاً: 20-80 ريال، 400-900 ريال)  
12. **رابط صورة** (من Unsplash أو Pexels - استخدم كلمات مفتاحية مثل "Makkah hotel", "Madinah cafe")  
    - تأكد أن الرابط يعمل وينتهي بـ .jpg أو .png  
13. **المميزات** (مثل: واي فاي مجاني، مواقف سيارات، اطلالة على الحرم)

## 📦 شكل الإخراج المطلوب:
أخرج البيانات في **كائن JSON واحد** كبير، يحتوي على 4 مصفوفات رئيسية:
- hotels
- restaurants
- cafes
- malls

وكل مصفوفة مقسمة إلى:  
- makkah  
- madinah

⚠️ تعليمات مهمة جدًا:
للروابط المعطلة أو 404: استخدم ذكاءك لاستنتاج إحداثيات المكان بناءً على اسمه وموقعه المعروف (مثلاً: "أبراج البيت" أو "جبل عمر").

للصور: استخدم روابط من Unsplash مع كلمات مفتاحية دقيقة.
مثال:
https://images.unsplash.com/photo-...
تأكد أن الرابط حقيقي ويعمل.

الترقيم: اجعل id يبدأ من 1 للفنادق، 101 للمطاعم، 201 للكافيهات، 301 للمولات (حتى لو ما فيه مولات حالياً).

الدقة: الإحداثيات يجب أن تكون قريبة من الواقع قدر الإمكان (±0.01).

المخرجات: فقط JSON صالح للقراءة، لا تكتب أي كلام إضافي خارج الكود.

الآن نفذ المهمة كاملة وأنشئ ملف JSON ضخم يحتوي على جميع الأماكن المذكورة أعلاه.

text

---

## 🚀 كيف تستخدمه؟

1. ادخل على [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)
2. اختر Gemini 1.5 Pro أو Flash
3. الصق البرومبت بالكامل في حقل التعليمات
4. شغّل النموذج
5. راح يطلع لك **ملف JSON كامل** فيه كل الفنادق والمطاعم والكافيهات
6. نسخ + لصق في ملف `data.json` واستخدمه في تطبيقك

---

## 🧠 ليش هذا البرومبت "ذكي"؟

✅ **يستنتج الإحداثيات** حتى لو الرابط ما يشتغل  
✅ **يولّد روابط صور** حقيقية من Unsplash  
✅ **يصنف تلقائياً** (فاخر، متوسط، اقتصادي)  
✅ **يقدّر التقييم والأسعار** حسب شهرة المكان  
✅ **يضيف وصف جذاب** لكل مكان  
✅ **مخرجات JSON جاهزة للاستخدام** بدون أي تعديل

**Expanding Data Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating rich location datasets for Makkah and Madinah into the application. My aim is to extract and structure data for Hotels, Restaurants, Cafes, and Malls, using JSON format. I'm also planning the user interface update, focusing on a new "Counter" tab for the UI/UX.


**Refining AI Integration**

I'm now integrating the "Rafeeq" AI assistant, aiming for seamless handling of fatwas, ritual explanations, and planning. I'm focusing on utilizing the official Saudi Ifta source, `https://www.alifta.gov.sa`, for religious rulings. I've also begun refining the AI logic with `services/geminiService.ts`. I’m concurrently working on the "Counter" tab's functionality.

payload.text: خل في مساحه بين اختر بلدك و البدء الان

**Analyzing UI Element Spacing**

I'm currently focused on the onboarding screen's layout within `App.tsx`. Specifically, I'm examining the spacing between the "Nationality" dropdown ("اختر بلدك") and the "Start Now" button ("البدء الآن"). I aim to adjust the UI component's spacing. I need to get this button to look just right.


**Refining Layout Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on the interaction between the select boxes and the "Start Now" button. The `mt-auto` class currently handles spacing. However, I am considering whether the desired "space between" might be more about spacing within the select container itself, rather than solely relying on the `mt-auto` margin on the button container.


**Revising Spacing Options**

I'm revisiting the spacing issue, considering the need for consistent visual separation, especially on smaller screens. I'm leaning toward adding `mt-10` to the button container to guarantee a minimum gap, even with `mt-auto`. Alternatively, a `pt-10` to the top container or a `py-8` to the bottom container might work. I will be sure to refine `App.tsx` and test the final result.


**Implementing Spacing Adjustment**

I've decided to add `pt-12` to the button container in `App.tsx` to achieve the necessary visual separation between the select boxes and the "Start Now" button. I'm confident this solution fulfills the user's request. I also will confirm that the other visual elements remain correct, specifically colors, and the "العداد" (Counter) location. I'm double-checking the code to ensure there are no unintended side effects.
